name: dynamo-trt
_comment_c_serving: 5 µs/seq with serving_overlap_factor=1.0 — lower end of the 5-22 µs C++/CUDA-graph + orchestrator range in decode.md §7.2, matching the panel-(a)/(b)/(c) validator defaults. Under CUDA-Graph replay (ρ_serving=1) the CPU pipelines per-seq host work (block-table gather, sampling, scheduler) for step N+1 during step N's GPU compute window, so the gross host cost t_serving,gross = c_serving·B hides under t_GPU_step until it exceeds the GPU window. The Dynamo orchestrator's CUDA-Graph absorption is the load-bearing reason c_serving sits at the low end of the docstring range — pure TRT-LLM without Dynamo (trt.json) takes 75 µs/seq, a 15× larger value reflecting the absence of orchestrator-level per-step bookkeeping absorption.
_comment_kernel_launch: 4.0 µs — Dynamo-orchestrator calibrated effective per-kernel cost. Sits between the CUDA-Graph optimum (1.5 µs) and TaxBreak's measured H100/H200 eager-mode floor (4.5-4.7 µs; arxiv 2603.12465). Production Dynamo+TRT at small B does not hit the CUDA-Graph optimum because (a) MoE-routing dispatch isn't fully captured by the graph, (b) the per-step scheduler-tick path runs outside the graph, and (c) the framework's flat kernels_per_layer_compute=10 under-counts MoE kernel fanout (TaxBreak: MoE models dispatch 8-11× more kernels per token than dense). The 4.0 µs value absorbs all three effects empirically; consistent with the DSr1 and GPT-OSS-120B Dynamo+TRT validators (DEFAULT_KERNEL_LAUNCH_US=4.0) and with dynamo_sglang.json (same orchestrator behavior).
_comment_moe_a2a_pattern: scatter — Dynamo+TRT exposes DeepEP-style scatter-direct dispatch (decode.md §5.2).
